description: OT threat actor profiles relevant to Malaysian NCII sectors. Includes nation-state groups with demonstrated OT capability and criminal groups targeting OT environments.
verificationNote: Threat actor profiles based on publicly available threat intelligence reports from Dragos, Mandiant, CrowdStrike, CISA advisories, and US DOJ indictments.

threatActors.name: Sandworm
threatActors.attribution: Russia — GRU (Main Intelligence Directorate) Military Unit 74455
threatActors.primaryObjective: Disruption of critical infrastructure for geopolitical effect
threatActors.relevanceToMalaysia: Low direct threat — primarily targets Ukraine and Western allies. Relevant as capability benchmark for other actors and for understanding what SL 3/4 OT attacks look like. Malaysian NCII operators in energy sector should understand Industroyer-class attack patterns for detection rule development.

threatActors.name: XENOTIME
threatActors.attribution: Russia — Central Scientific Research Institute of Chemistry and Mechanics (TsNIIKhM), per US DOJ
threatActors.capability: SL 4 — unique safety system targeting capability
demonstratedCapability: TRITON/TRISIS attack on Schneider Triconex SIS (2017)
Demonstrated reconnaissance of US and Australian water/electric/oil infrastructure
threatActors.relevanceToMalaysia: Relevant to Malaysian petrochemical sector (Petronas, refining facilities). Any Malaysian operator running Triconex or other Schneider SIS should review Triconex-specific hardening guides and ensure SIS network isolation.

threatActors.name: VOLTZITE / APT40
threatActors.attribution: China — Ministry of State Security (MSS), Hainan Province
threatActors.capability: SL 3 — sophisticated OT reconnaissance and access
threatActors.primaryObjective: Strategic intelligence collection; pre-positioning for potential destructive operations
demonstratedCapability: Targeting of utilities and critical infrastructure in US Pacific and ASEAN region
OT network reconnaissance and long-term persistent access
threatActors.relevanceToMalaysia: High relevance — Malaysia is within ASEAN region targeted by APT40/VOLTZITE. Malaysian maritime, energy, and government OT environments are likely within scope of Chinese intelligence collection interest. NACSA-designated NCII entities in energy, maritime, and government sectors should consider this threat in their SL-T assignment.

threatActors.name: KAMACITE / APT28-affiliated
threatActors.attribution: Russia — GRU-linked, associated with Sandworm staging operations
threatActors.primaryObjective: Initial access and persistence in energy sector OT environments for Sandworm handoff
demonstratedCapability: Credential collection campaigns against energy sector
threatActors.relevanceToMalaysia: Low-medium — primarily targeting European and North American energy. Technique profile is relevant for defence planning.

threatActors.name: Ransomware Groups (OT-targeting)
threatActors.attribution: Financially motivated criminal groups — primarily Eastern Europe
threatActors.capability: SL 2 — IT-first with OT impact through IT/OT connectivity
threatActors.primaryObjective: Financial extortion via ransomware — leverage OT criticality to maximise ransom demand
threatActors.specificOtCapability: EKANS/SNAKE malware specifically terminates OT-related Windows services (SCADA, historian, GE Digital, Honeywell, OSIsoft PI) before encryption to maximise OT disruption and ransom leverage
threatActors.relevanceToMalaysia: High — Malaysian manufacturing, energy, and transport sectors are within active targeting scope of financially motivated ransomware groups. The IT/OT convergence common in Malaysian industrial facilities creates the lateral movement path these groups exploit.

threatActors.name: Sandworm (Industroyer2 operations)
threatActors.attribution: Russia — GRU, Sandworm sub-group responsible for Industroyer2 deployment
threatActors.primaryObjective: Destructive attacks on power grid infrastructure
demonstratedCapability: Industroyer2 (Ukraine, 2022) — updated Industroyer malware targeting IEC 104 RTUs
threatActors.relevanceToMalaysia: Low direct threat. Techniques relevant for TNB and energy sector SCADA monitoring rule development.

threatActors.name: Opportunistic Attackers — Internet-Exposed OT
threatActors.attribution: Unknown / varied — global opportunistic threat actors
threatActors.primaryObjective: Opportunistic access, ransomware, defacement, or hacktivism
demonstratedCapability: Shodan-driven exploitation of default credentials on internet-exposed SCADA/HMI
Opportunistic ransomware affecting OT environments via IT pivot
Script kiddie defacement of water treatment HMI displays (multiple incidents globally)
threatActors.relevanceToMalaysia: Very High — multiple Malaysian industrial facilities have internet-exposed OT interfaces discoverable via Shodan. Any OT system with a public IP and weak/default credentials is an active target. This is the most likely threat for the majority of Malaysian OT operators who are not NCII-designated.
